methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series E → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.4x) to $400M last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 6 same-sector + same-stage peers in AI & Software. Range: $1.6B - $250.0B.

data.narrative:
Modal is a Series E AI & Software company seeking valuation guidance. The peer universe consists of 9 comparables: 1 public (Palantir) and 8 private firms including xAI, Crusoe Energy, Groq, SandboxAQ, and Ayar Labs operating in the same sector and stage.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis uses Modal's last post-money valuation of $400M and applies a 1.4x step-up multiplier typical for Series E progression, yielding $560M. Peer-median analysis examines six same-sector, same-stage comparables with a median valuation of $6.3B, though the peer set exhibits substantial dispersion: minimum $1.6B (Ayar Labs), maximum $250B (xAI), reflecting divergent assessments of AI frontier-tech risk and market opportunity.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $476M to $7.2B, with a median implied value of $3.4B. The wide spread between stage-progression ($560M) and peer-median ($6.3B) reflects fundamental uncertainty around comparable relevance. Modal's infrastructure-software positioning and $80M total capital raised suggest it occupies the lower-to-middle portion of the peer range rather than the xAI outlier. Key uncertainties include product differentiation relative to peers, customer traction metrics, and whether the peer set adequately captures frontier-AI infrastructure risk. A valuation in the $1.5B–$4B range appears most defensible pending deeper due diligence on unit economics and retention.
